source_name: Code4rena Angle Findings and Analysis Report (2023-06)
finding_title: estimatedAPR() might return the wrong APR
summary: estimatedAPR() might return the wrong APR and it will make users confused.
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in estimatedAPR() might return the wrong APR, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: the collateral management path must not allow the condition described by "estimatedAPR() might return the wrong APR" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: estimatedAPR() might return the wrong APR and it will make users confused.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; exploitability, affected value, or preconditions are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: estimatedAPR() should return 0 when lockedProfit() == 0. function estimatedAPR() external view returns (uint256 apr) { if (lockedProfit() == 0) return 0; //check locked profit first uint256 currentlyVestingProfit = vestingProfit; uint256 weightedAssets = vestingPeriod * totalAssets(); if (currentlyVestingProfit != 0 && weightedAssets != 0) apr = (currentlyVestingProfit * 3600 * 24 * 365 * BASE_18) / weightedAssets; }
